name: California

examFacts.notes: Administered by the California Department of Real Estate (DRE).

intro:
The California real estate salesperson exam is one of the most challenging in the country. California uses a single unified test — 150 questions over three hours, with no separate national and state portions — and every question can blend general real estate principles with California-specific law. You can't just memorize the national content and expect to pass.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. California's community property rules, Proposition 13 and 19 tax limitations, agency disclosure requirements, the Department of Real Estate's enforcement powers, earthquake- and coastal-zone disclosures, and the Real Estate Recovery Fund all show up on the exam — and they're nothing like what you'd see studying for a neighboring state.

Below are 20 free practice questions drawn from RealReady's full California bank. Each one tests a specific topic, with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation tucked under the card. Work through them honestly: commit to an answer first, then reveal. The ones you miss tell you exactly where to focus next.

For the rest of California's question bank — plus progress tracking, missed-question mode, and category-level analytics — get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android.

faq.q: How many questions are on the California real estate exam?
faq.a: The California salesperson exam has 150 multiple-choice questions. The DRE administers it as a single unified test — there's no separate national portion. You have three hours to complete it.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the California real estate exam?
faq.a: 70%, which is 105 of 150 questions correct. The DRE doesn't curve and there's no partial credit, so every question matters.

faq.q: How much does the California real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The salesperson examination fee is $60 per attempt. If you pass, the salesperson license fee is an additional $245, and the license is valid for four years before renewal.

faq.q: How long is the California real estate exam?
faq.a: Three hours (180 minutes) for the salesperson exam. The DRE administers it at testing centers across the state, and you'll find out whether you passed before you leave.

faq.q: Do I have to take a pre-license course before the California exam?
faq.a: Yes. California requires 135 hours of pre-license education — Real Estate Principles, Real Estate Practice, and one elective from an approved list — at a DRE-approved school. You also have to be fingerprinted via Live Scan before the DRE will issue your license.
